content.json:
# AVD Orphaned Session Host Detector

This workbook identifies **orphaned Azure Virtual Desktop session hosts** — VMs that appear to be AVD-related but are **not currently registered** to any host pool.

### Detection Criteria
A VM is considered an AVD candidate if **any** of the following are true:
- VM name contains the filter value (case-insensitive)
- Resource group name contains the filter value (case-insensitive)
- Any tag key or value contains the filter value (case-insensitive)

VMs with `updater` in their name are treated as **master images** and shown in a dedicated section.

> This workbook queries across **all subscriptions** your account has access to via Azure Resource Graph.

content.query:
resources
| where type =~ "microsoft.compute/virtualmachines"
| where tolower(name) contains tolower("{NameFilter}")
    or tolower(resourceGroup) contains tolower("{NameFilter}")
    or tolower(tostring(tags)) contains tolower("{NameFilter}")
| where not(tolower(name) contains "updater")
| count
| project Count, Label = "🔎 Candidates"

desktopvirtualizationresources
| where type =~ "microsoft.desktopvirtualization/hostpools/sessionhosts"
| count
| project Count, Label = "✅ Registered"

resources
| where type =~ "microsoft.compute/virtualmachines"
| where tolower(name) contains tolower("{NameFilter}")
    or tolower(resourceGroup) contains tolower("{NameFilter}")
    or tolower(tostring(tags)) contains tolower("{NameFilter}")
| where not(tolower(name) contains "updater")
| project vmId = tolower(id)
| join kind=leftouter (
    desktopvirtualizationresources
    | where type =~ "microsoft.desktopvirtualization/hostpools/sessionhosts"
    | extend vmResourceId = tolower(tostring(properties.resourceId))
    | project vmResourceId
) on $left.vmId == $right.vmResourceId
| where isempty(vmResourceId)
| count
| project Count, Label = "⚠️ Orphaned"

desktopvirtualizationresources
| where type =~ "microsoft.desktopvirtualization/hostpools/sessionhosts"
| extend status = tostring(properties.status)
| where status in~ ("Unavailable", "NeedsAssistance")
| count
| project Count, Label = "🟠 Unhealthy"

content.json:
---
## Orphaned VMs
These VMs match AVD naming/tagging criteria but are **not registered** as session hosts in any host pool. They may be costing you money without serving any AVD workload. VMs with `updater` in their name are excluded here — see the **Master Images** section below.

> **Recommended actions:** Verify each VM — if it's no longer needed, deallocate and delete it. If it should be registered, re-register it to the appropriate host pool.

content.query:
resources
| where type =~ "microsoft.compute/virtualmachines"
| where tolower(name) contains tolower("{NameFilter}")
    or tolower(resourceGroup) contains tolower("{NameFilter}")
    or tolower(tostring(tags)) contains tolower("{NameFilter}")
| where not(tolower(name) contains "updater")
| project vmId = tolower(id), vmName = name, resourceGroup, subscriptionId, location,
    vmSize = tostring(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize),
    osType = tostring(properties.storageProfile.osDisk.osType),
    tags,
    id
| join kind=leftouter (
    desktopvirtualizationresources
    | where type =~ "microsoft.desktopvirtualization/hostpools/sessionhosts"
    | extend vmResourceId = tolower(tostring(properties.resourceId))
    | project vmResourceId
) on $left.vmId == $right.vmResourceId
| where isempty(vmResourceId)
| project-away vmId, vmResourceId
| sort by resourceGroup asc, vmName asc

content.json:
---
## Registered Session Hosts
All session hosts currently registered to host pools. Use this to verify healthy registrations and spot hosts in a bad state.

content.query:
desktopvirtualizationresources
| where type =~ "microsoft.desktopvirtualization/hostpools/sessionhosts"
| extend idParts = split(id, '/')
| extend hostPoolName = tostring(idParts[8])
| extend rgName = tostring(idParts[4])
| extend status = tostring(properties.status)
| extend updateState = tostring(properties.updateState)
| extend lastHeartbeat = todatetime(properties.lastHeartbeat)
| extend assignedUser = tostring(properties.assignedUser)
| extend vmResourceId = tolower(tostring(properties.resourceId))
| project hostPoolName, sessionHost = name, status, updateState, lastHeartbeat,
    assignedUser, rgName, vmResourceId
| sort by status asc, hostPoolName asc, sessionHost asc

content.json:
---
## Unhealthy Registered Hosts
Session hosts that **are** registered and explicitly in problematic states: **Unavailable** or **NeedsAssistance**. This avoids flagging intentionally **Shutdown** hosts (for autoscaling/maintenance).

content.query:
desktopvirtualizationresources
| where type =~ "microsoft.desktopvirtualization/hostpools/sessionhosts"
| extend status = tostring(properties.status)
| where status in~ ("Unavailable", "NeedsAssistance")
| extend idParts = split(id, '/')
| extend hostPoolName = tostring(idParts[8])
| extend rgName = tostring(idParts[4])
| extend lastHeartbeat = todatetime(properties.lastHeartbeat)
| extend updateState = tostring(properties.updateState)
| extend assignedUser = tostring(properties.assignedUser)
| extend vmResourceId = tolower(tostring(properties.resourceId))
| project hostPoolName, sessionHost = name, status, updateState, lastHeartbeat,
    assignedUser, rgName, vmResourceId
| sort by lastHeartbeat asc

content.json:
---
## Ghost Registrations
Session hosts registered in the AVD control plane where the underlying VM **no longer exists**. This is the reverse orphan — a stale registration pointing at a deleted VM. These should be removed from the host pool.

content.query:
desktopvirtualizationresources
| where type =~ "microsoft.desktopvirtualization/hostpools/sessionhosts"
| extend vmResourceId = tolower(tostring(properties.resourceId))
| extend idParts = split(id, '/')
| extend hostPoolName = tostring(idParts[8])
| extend status = tostring(properties.status)
| extend lastHeartbeat = todatetime(properties.lastHeartbeat)
| project vmResourceId, hostPoolName, sessionHost = name, status, lastHeartbeat, shId = id
| join kind=leftouter (
    resources
    | where type =~ "microsoft.compute/virtualmachines"
    | project vmId = tolower(id)
) on $left.vmResourceId == $right.vmId
| where isempty(vmId)
| project-away vmResourceId, vmId
| sort by hostPoolName asc, sessionHost asc

content.json:
---
## Master Images (Updater VMs)
VMs with `updater` in their name that match AVD criteria. These are typically master/gold images used for host pool updates and should **not** be left running or missing auto-shutdown schedules.

Rows are flagged if the VM is **powered on** or has **no auto-shutdown schedule** configured.

content.query:
resources
| where type =~ "microsoft.compute/virtualmachines"
| where tolower(name) contains tolower("{NameFilter}")
    or tolower(resourceGroup) contains tolower("{NameFilter}")
    or tolower(tostring(tags)) contains tolower("{NameFilter}")
| where tolower(name) contains "updater"
| extend powerState = tostring(properties.extended.instanceView.powerState.displayStatus)
| extend vmSchedName = tolower(strcat("shutdown-computevm-", name))
| project vmSchedName, vmName = name, resourceGroup, subscriptionId, location,
    vmSize = tostring(properties.hardwareProfile.vmSize),
    powerState, tags, id
| join kind=leftouter (
    resources
    | where type =~ "microsoft.devtestlab/schedules"
    | extend schedStatus = tostring(properties.status)
    | project schedName = tolower(name), schedStatus
) on $left.vmSchedName == $right.schedName
| extend isRunning = (powerState contains "running")
| extend autoShutdown = iff(isempty(schedStatus), "Not Configured", schedStatus)
| extend needsAttention = iff(isRunning,
    iff(isempty(schedStatus), "Running + No Auto-Shutdown", "Running"),
    iff(isempty(schedStatus), "No Auto-Shutdown",
        iff(schedStatus =~ "Enabled", "", "Auto-Shutdown Disabled")))
| project vmName, resourceGroup, needsAttention, powerState, autoShutdown, subscriptionId, location, vmSize, tags, id
| sort by needsAttention desc, vmName asc
